title: Efficient Attention Variants
summary: The $O(n^2)$ attention bottleneck limits sequence length. Solutions: sparse attention (attend to subset), linear attention (kernel trick removes softmax), Flash Attention (IO-aware implementation, exact but fast). Flash Attention is the dominant practical solution.
key_insights: Standard attention: $O(n^2 d)$ compute, $O(n^2)$ memory for score matrix. At $n = 8192$: 67M entries per head per layer.
Sparse attention: only compute attention for a subset of positions. Fixed patterns (local window + global tokens) or learned.
Linear attention: replace $\text{softmax}(QK^\top)V$ with $\phi(Q)(\phi(K)^\top V)$. Associativity trick: $O(nd^2)$ instead of $O(n^2 d)$. But quality drops.
Flash Attention: exact standard attention, but reorders computation to minimize HBM reads/writes. ~2-4× faster, uses $O(n)$ memory via tiling.
Flash Attention insight: the bottleneck is memory bandwidth, not FLOPs. GPU SRAM is fast but small. Tile the computation to stay in SRAM.
KV-cache: at inference, cache K/V from previous tokens. New token only computes one Q row. Reduces autoregressive cost from $O(n^2)$ to $O(n)$ per token.

mathematical_core.sparse_local: Window of $w$ neighbors: $O(nwd)$ compute. Longformer: local + global tokens.
mathematical_core.linear: $\text{Attn}(Q,K,V) = \phi(Q)(\phi(K)^\top V)$. Compute $\phi(K)^\top V \in \mathbb{R}^{d \times d}$ first, then multiply by $\phi(Q)$. $O(nd^2)$.
mathematical_core.flash_attention: Same $O(n^2 d)$ FLOPs as standard, but $O(n)$ memory and ~2-4× wall-clock speedup via tiling.
mathematical_core.kv_cache: Inference: cache $K_{1:t}, V_{1:t}$. Token $t+1$: compute $Q_{t+1}$, append $K_{t+1}, V_{t+1}$ to cache. Attention cost: $O(td)$ not $O(t^2 d)$.

derivations.title: Flash Attention — IO complexity analysis

steps.name: Standard attention IO
steps.math: Write full $n \times n$ score matrix to HBM, read it back for softmax, write result. HBM reads/writes: $O(n^2)$.

steps.name: Flash Attention tiling
steps.math: Process $Q, K, V$ in blocks that fit in SRAM. Compute partial softmax per block, combine using online softmax trick. Never materialize full $n \times n$ matrix.

steps.name: Result
steps.math: HBM reads/writes: $O(n^2 d / M)$ where $M$ = SRAM size. Since $M \gg d$: much fewer memory accesses. Same FLOPs, fewer memory ops → faster.

derivations.consequence: Flash Attention is the dominant implementation. It's exact (no approximation), faster, and uses less memory. Practically eliminates the need for approximate attention methods.

related: TRANSFORMER.MASKING — causal mask integrates with Flash Attention
PRODUCTION.QUANTIZATION — quantized attention further reduces memory

notes: Flash Attention makes the $O(n^2)$ concern less acute for sequences up to ~32K. Beyond that: sparse or hybrid approaches still needed.
